page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it bundles eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 180 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not included in this configuration, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution is highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks ranking in the top 25%. The instance excels in large-scale parallel execution, showing strong memory read and write bandwidth alongside high-throughput Redis SET operations.

The integrated NVIDIA L4 GPUs deliver exceptional performance for machine learning workloads. LLM inference benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as text generation for medium-sized models. Text generation for large 70B models remains strong, placing in the top 25%. Conversely, standard CPU-bound tasks like single-threaded compression and text processing show lower relative efficiency.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-96 offers high resource density for GPU-accelerated workloads, trading off local storage and single-core CPU speed for massive parallel processing capacity. This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale LLM inference, machine learning training, multi-threaded data processing, and GPU-accelerated rendering tasks, while being less optimal for single-threaded legacy applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory and bundles eight NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPUs with a total of 180 GB of VRAM. While single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression benchmarks are relatively weak, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance and top-tier LLM inference speeds, particularly for prompt processing. Local storage is not included. This server is highly suited for parallelized workloads, machine learning inference, and GPU-accelerated data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 384GB RAM, and 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs, 384GB RAM, and 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, optimized for high-throughput machine learning inference.
tagline: A high-density GPU accelerator platform featuring eight Ada Lovelace GPUs and ninety-six vCPUs for demanding machine learning inference workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPUs with 180 GB total VRAM
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
384.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier benchmark performance for large language model inference
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities
Designed for machine learning, parallel data processing, and GPU rendering
